message:

+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "valkey-2019-bug-valkey-server-slows-down-with-spop-key-and-srandmember-key-spinn",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "valkey: [BUG] valkey-server slows down with `spop <key>` and `srandmember <key>` spinning in hashtableScanDefrag after a few hours",
+    "description": "[BUG] valkey-server slows down with `spop <key>` and `srandmember <key>` spinning in hashtableScanDefrag after a few hours. This issue affects 7+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify valkey troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your valkey installation:
```bash
valkey-server --version
valkey-cli ping
```
Look for errors or warnings that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review valkey configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant valkey configuration:
After a few hours of uptime, one of our valkey-server processes (Docker `8.1.1-alpine` and `8.1.1` images) starts consuming 100% CPU usage and only sporadically responds to application requests."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for [BUG] valkey-server slows down with `spop <key>` and…",
+        "description": "Tested with starting Valkey with `activedefrag no` instead, I observed the process staying at ~30% CPU usage for about 4 hours and then quickly ramp up to 100% within a few (~10) minutes.

The same set now shows this information:
```
127.0.0.1:38542> debug object urls:unretrievable
Value
```yaml

```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm [BUG] valkey-server slows down with `spop <key>`… is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
Test valkey to confirm the issue is resolved.
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],

+      "summary": "Tested with starting Valkey with `activedefrag no` instead, I observed the process staying at ~30% CPU usage for about 4 hours and then quickly ramp up to 100% within a few (~10) minutes.",

--activedefrag yes
--aof-timestamp-enabled yes
--appendfsync everysec
--appendonly yes
--bind ::
--bind 0.0.0.0
--client-output-buffer-limit normal 1gb 16mb 15
--client-output-buffer-limit pubsub 64mb 8mb 15
--client-output-buffer-limit replica 16gb 1gb 300
--client-query-buffer-limit 512mb
--databases 1
--dual-channel-replication-enabled yes
--enable-debug-command local
--enable-module-command local
--enable-protected-configs local
--hide-user-data-from-log no
--io-threads 8
--log-format logfmt
--loglevel verbose
--log-timestamp-format iso8601
--maxmemory 128gb
--maxmemory-clients 1%
--maxmemory-policy noeviction
--max-new-connections-per-cycle 100
--no-appendfsync-on-rewrite yes
--oom-score-adj relative
--port ${PORT}
--proto-max-bulk-len 512mb
--repl-backlog-size 64mb
--re",
